person_name: Anicia Faltonia Proba, Roman Christian noblewoman (c. 360–c. 432)
person_name_full: Anicia Faltonia Proba, Roman Christian noblewoman (c. 360–c. 432)
notes: Roman Christian noblewoman known for her holy life and charity; mother of consuls Probinus, Olybrius, and Probus.

docs.title: Letter CXXX
docs.author: Jerome
docs.excerpt: … bears the most illustrious name, whose holy life and universal charity have won for her esteem even among the barbarians, who has made nothing of the regular consulships enjoyed by her three sons, Probinus, Olybrius, and Probus,--that Proba, I say, now that Rome has been taken and its contents

docs.title: Letter 130
docs.author: Jerome
